 {"id":"bd-6tme.2","title":"Adoption wedge: deterministic MVCC snapshot isolation demo","description":"# Adoption wedge: deterministic MVCC snapshot isolation demo

## GOAL
Single-command demo showing MVCC snapshot isolation on a filesystem: two concurrent readers see consistent snapshots while a writer modifies files. The adoption wedge for the MVCC story.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `cargo run --example mvcc_demo` -- creates image, starts 2 read transactions + 1 write transaction, shows isolation
2. Output shows: reader A sees version 1, writer commits version 2, reader A still sees version 1, reader B (started after commit) sees version 2
3. No FUSE mount needed -- operates on raw image via API
4. Deterministic, < 3 seconds

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: demo exits 0 with expected output pattern
2. Integration: snapshot isolation invariant verified

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Transaction start (info): txn_id, snapshot_seq
- Read (debug): txn_id, block, version_seen
- Write commit (info): txn_id, commit_seq, blocks_written
- Isolation check (info): reader_txn, expected_version, actual_version, isolated=true/false

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Single command, < 3 seconds
2. Demonstrates snapshot isolation visually
3. Deterministic
4. Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6tme.3","title":"E2E test: self-healing demo runs successfully and produces correct output","description":"# E2E test: self-healing demo runs successfully and produces correct output

## GOAL
E2E test for the Killer Demo epic (bd-6tme). Runs the demo script end-to-end and verifies the output format, repair correctness, and timing.

## TEST SCENARIO
1. Run `cargo run --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing` (or equivalent)
2. Verify exit code 0
3. Parse output: verify corruption count, repair count, verification pass
4. Verify: corrupted blocks == repaired blocks (zero data loss)
5. Verify: evidence ledger present and parseable
6. Verify: total time < 30 seconds

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Demo runs without error
2. All corrupted blocks repaired
3. Output matches expected format
4. Timing within bounds
5. Deterministic with fixed seed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueElm","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:30.417390920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T22:27:41.312923975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T22:11:09.385271199Z","close_reason":"Completed: self-healing demo E2E harness passes with rch and validates output/repair/timing/determinism/evidence-ledger","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","e2e","selfhealing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tme.3","depends_on_id":"bd-6tme","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:30.417390920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6tme.3","depends_on_id":"bd-6tme.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:52.365343179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-6tme.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-78sz","title":"Implement ext4 inline data read support","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:11:55.703345481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:12:08.299466470Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:12:08.298942226Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 inline data read support: (1) Added read_ext4_inline_data() method that reads file content from inode extent_bytes (60 bytes) + xattr_ibody continuation.  {"id":"bd-79ni","title":"Harden all log contract tests across workspace against tracing callsite poisoning","description":"## What
Final verification that the tracing callsite fix is complete, plus minor cleanup.

## Status: 95% DONE — VERIFICATION + TYPO FIX ONLY

### Already Done
- ffs-cli: 6 log contract tests use Dispatch::new + set_default + rebuild_interest_cache
  directly (inline pattern, no helper function). 4 non-log-contract tests serialized via
  log_contract_guard(). EnvFilter removed from test imports.
- ffs-core: 10+ log contract tests use install_json_subscriber() helper.
  Doc comment exists at line 15417-15419 (but has typo: line 15418 says "/ " not "///").
- Zero remaining with_default + EnvFilter in any test code (verified by grep).

### Remaining Work
1. Fix typo in ffs-core install_json_subscriber doc comment (line 15418: "/ " → "///")
2. Run cargo test --workspace 5 consecutive times to prove zero flakes
3. Close bead

## Test Plan
- cargo test --workspace × 5, zero failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Doc comment typo fixed
- [ ] 5 consecutive clean test runs
- [ ] Bead closed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:52.859989786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:04:11.511090988Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:04:11.510829654Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7a45","title":"Verify btrfs RAID56 parity rotation against upstream","description":"Current resolve_raid56_stripe parity rotation in crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs needs validation vs kernel (legacy corpus missing). Confirm rotation formula and adjust tests accordingly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-13T18:13:51.341820284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:42:07.561390886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:42:07.557440586Z","close_reason":"Verified RAID56 parity rotation is correct. Implementation uses left-symmetric layout: P starts at last device (num-1) and rotates left (decreasing index) each stripe row. Q follows P at adjacent position with wraparound. This was fixed in commit 862b145 and test stripe_resolve_raid6_parity_wraparound validates the behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","parity"]}
